meaning: adj.[鍝瞉 瀛樺湪璁虹殑,鏈綋璁虹殑adj.[鍝瞉鏈綋璁虹殑,瀹炰綋璁虹殑
examples: Transform an arbitrary REST result into a generic ontological individual.
In the fourth Way, Aquinas offers a version of the Ontological Argument (see Anselm).
There's no ontological subordination of the objects properties to the object itself.
There was initially no ontological gulf between the world of the gods and the world of men and women.
It seems to be a human imperative to stretch ontological boundaries and strive for greater levels of detail.
